description.task: Implement a VersionConflictResolver class that models Maven's nearest-wins and Gradle's highest-wins version resolution strategies. Given conflicting versions of the same dependency artifact, return the resolved version for each strategy.
instructions: resolveNearest() finds the candidate with the minimum depth value
On tie in depth, resolveNearest() returns the first occurrence in the list
resolveHighest() compares versions numerically: compare major, then minor, then patch
parseVersion() helper splits the version string and converts to int array
Both methods must handle a list with a single candidate correctly
description.hint: For resolveNearest(), use stream min with Comparator.comparingInt(c -> c.depth()). For resolveHighest(), compare int arrays element by element using Arrays.compare().

description.task: Create a TransitiveDependencyExpander class that resolves the full transitive dependency tree of a project. Given direct dependencies and their own dependencies, expand the full set of libraries needed at runtime.
instructions: register() stores each artifact's direct dependencies in dependencyGraph
expandAll() uses BFS or DFS starting from directDeps
The result includes the directDeps themselves and all their transitive dependencies
Each artifact appears only once in the result set (no duplicates)
Artifacts not registered in dependencyGraph are treated as leaf nodes with no further dependencies
description.hint: Use a Queue for BFS. Start by adding all directDeps to both the queue and result. For each polled artifact, look up its deps in dependencyGraph, add any not yet in result, and enqueue them.

description.task: Implement a BomEnforcer class that simulates Maven's Bill of Materials (BOM) concept. A BOM defines approved versions for artifacts. The enforcer checks whether project dependencies use BOM-approved versions and reports any violations.
instructions: importBom() merges the given map into bomVersions
enforceVersions() iterates over projectDeps; for each artifact in bomVersions, check if version matches
Violations are reported only when the project specifies a non-empty version that differs from the BOM
Violation message format: 'VIOLATION: [artifact] uses [project version] but BOM requires [bom version]'
resolveVersions() fills empty project versions from the BOM; artifacts not in BOM are returned as-is
description.hint: In enforceVersions(), check if !projVersion.isEmpty() && !projVersion.equals(bomVersion) to trigger violation. In resolveVersions(), use bomVersions.getOrDefault() to fill in blanks.
description.expectedOutput:
[VIOLATION: spring-core uses 5.3.0 but BOM requires 6.0.0]
jackson resolved to: 2.14.0
